methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Luma AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Runway
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Magic AI, a Series C stage AI & Software company founded by Eric Steinberger and Sebastian De Ro, is evaluated against a peer set comprising 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private frontier-tech firms in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $1.5B last post-money valuation and applies a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implying $2.5B. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks against 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Luma AI at $4.0B, and Runway at $1.5B. The median across this cohort is $9.0B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B.

Synthesizing both methods yields a valuation range of $2.2B (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.8B. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—driven principally by heterogeneous business models and development stage within Series C—represents the primary uncertainty. Magic AI's position within this range depends on traction metrics, capital efficiency relative to peers, and product-market fit trajectory, none of which are quantified in available data. The Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively at $2.5B, while the Peer-Median method reflects the sector's current valuation expansion, suggesting fair value likely falls between $5B and $8B absent material new information on unit economics or revenue.
